method: CV of first-row x-extent vs assembly null (pooled widths+gaps at actual counts); perm_p=frac(null CV<=obs CV); positive control fixed-extent detect + random-assembly false-positive.
key_findings: obs first-row x-extent CV=0.4065 vs assembly-null median 0.5445 (ratio 0.747), perm_p=0.00050 -> the real header writing extent is significantly MORE constant than random width+gap assembly at the actual per-doc counts.
extent/doc-x-extent median ratio = 0.842 -> the header row occupies ~84% of the document writing width (near-full-width), consistent with a bounded writing area (per E086).
Positive control PASSED: fixed-extent detect power_est=1.00 (detect_p=0.0005); random-assembly false-positive rate=0.100 (<=0.10 gate; stable 0.067-0.100 across seeds).
n_docs=140 usable HT docs (>=6 non-divider bbox glyphs, >=2 rows); pools of 628 first-row widths and 488 inter-glyph gaps.

Mechanism: a bounded writing WIDTH (not a bounded ink/area budget, which E088 rejected) is the likely driver of E087's heading-size<->header-length tradeoff: bigger first-row glyphs mechanically leave room for fewer within a near-fixed horizontal extent.
successor_hypotheses: E090: Does the constrained extent scale with TABLET support width (compare Tablet vs other supports) to separate tablet-dimension from scribal-convention explanations.
E091: Within fixed-extent docs, is per-glyph width the free variable (count*width ~ const) or is count fixed and width absorbs slack.
E092: Does the same width constraint hold for non-header (body) rows, or is it header-specific.
E093: Combine width-constraint with E087 size<->count: predict header count from (target_extent / mean_glyph_width) and test residual structure.
E094: Test whether inter-glyph gaps shrink as glyph count rises (compression to fit fixed width) vs gaps staying constant.
E095: Cross-site replication of the extent-CV-below-null signature on non-HT Linear A corpora.
non_circular: Geometry-only (bbox positions/widths); opaque doc IDs; no semantic sign values used; metric and null derived from spatial extent, independent of E087's size<->count correlation.
deviations: Doc x-extent computed from all non-divider glyph bboxes (no doc-level width field present in source JSON); used for extent/doc ratio only, not for the verdict metric.
PC false-positive arm draws random counts uniformly in [min,max] of observed counts (random-assembly definition per protocol).
PC used 30 reps per arm (protocol floor is >=15) for a stable fire-rate estimate; independent RNG seeds per PC arm to avoid cross-arm RNG-state coupling (math unchanged).
honest_caveat: A constrained extent may reflect the TABLET physical dimension OR a scribal writing-area convention. The finding is reported as 'writing extent is constrained', NOT 'scribes deliberately targeted a width'.
